idea-plugin.name: Spring Boot Endpoint Explorer
idea-plugin.description:
Spring Boot Endpoint Explorer

Scans Spring Boot projects and displays all REST API endpoints in a dedicated tool window. No dependency resolution required — works with pure text-based annotation matching.


Features:

  • ✅ Scans @RestController / @Controller annotated classes
  • ✅ Groups endpoints by controller with base path and endpoint count
  • ✅ Shows colored HTTP method badges (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH)
  • ✅ Displays request parameters, path variables, request body, and return types
  • ✅ Double-click to navigate directly to endpoint source code
  • ✅ Real-time search filter by path, method name, or HTTP method
  • ✅ One-click refresh after code changes
  • ✅ Skips JDK and library files — scans only project source code
  • ✅ Configurable custom annotations via Settings dialog

Custom Annotations Support

If your project uses custom annotations (e.g., @MyController, @MyGetMapping:GET), click the gear icon in the toolbar to configure them. Add your annotation names and they will be recognized on the next scan.


Usage:

  1. Install the plugin and restart IntelliJ IDEA
  2. Open any Spring Boot project
  3. Click "Spring Endpoints" tab on the right tool window
  4. Click the Refresh button to scan all controllers
  5. Double-click any endpoint to jump to its source code
idea-plugin.change-notes:
v1.0.0

  • Initial release
  • Scan and display Spring Boot REST endpoints
  • Source code navigation by double-click
  • Real-time search and filter
  • Custom annotation configuration
  • Native IntelliJ ActionToolbar UI
